threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'The Tube' instead of just 'the Overground'—people here are big on their train lines.
Last night on the 133, a guy serenaded us with 'Wonderwall' using a peanut butter jar as a mic. Brixton's late-night vibes never disappoint.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I caught sight of the elderly lady at the bakery, sharing fresh bread with the street cats every morning. That’s when I truly felt at home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop where the owner doubles as a DJ, spinning tunes while serving up döner at 3 AM. It’s a vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they instinctively duck when walking past the BART entrance on a Friday evening. Always a good reason to be cautious.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Every time I pass Civic Center, I can’t help but wonder if the dog on the tracks is part of some urban art project gone rogue.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is all about fancy boutiques, but the real charm is in the tiny, bustling corners where locals still gather for a simple café amb llet.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a newbie by their bewildered look staring at the bus schedule. They still expect everything to run on time.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still avoid the Dalston Junction steps, thinking they'll take you somewhere nice. Just wait till you discover the shortcuts all the locals use.
Caught a night bus from Brixton last week; the driver serenaded us with '80s hits while navigating those potholes. Best ride ever!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: First time I saw a group of kids scootering past a street art mural while an older man sold homemade jam from a cart. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find late-night kebab shops blasting techno, where the smell of grilled meat mingles with thumping bass. Pure Kreuzberg vibes.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from the Mission if they can spot the difference between a taco truck’s carne asada and that frozen stuff from the grocery store.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy with a giant inflatable taco trying to board the 5-Fulton at Civic Center. Muni drivers didn’t bat an eye, just waved him on.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just grab a quick bite at any bar, but the tapas rush hour leaves no room for casual dining. It's a whole event.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they order their coffee—still asking for 'with milk' instead of a cortado. Classic rookie mistake.
